title: pittosporum leratii
intro: Pittosporum leratii, also known as Kōhia, is a small evergreen tree native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and bark are used in herbal preparations to promote respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Traditional uses include the preparation of teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
